sections.title: Overview
sections.content: The ActiveCampaign connector syncs contacts, deals, and campaigns from ActiveCampaign into the Shielva knowledge base using the ActiveCampaign REST API v3. Authentication uses an API key passed in the Api-Token request header. The connector implements offset-based pagination (using meta.total), exponential backoff retry, and a circuit breaker to ensure reliable, throttle-safe data ingestion. Each synced record receives a stable 16-character source_id derived from SHA-256(type:id)[:16].

subsections.title: API Key
subsections.content: ActiveCampaign uses a static API key passed as the Api-Token header on every request. The key is found in Settings → Developer in your ActiveCampaign account. API keys do not expire unless regenerated manually.
steps: Navigate to Developer in the left menu.
Copy the API Key shown below the URL.
Paste both into the connector install form in Shielva.

fields.label: API URL
fields.description: Your ActiveCampaign account API URL. Found in Settings → Developer → API URL. Do not append /api/3/ — the connector adds it automatically.

fields.label: API Key
fields.description: Your ActiveCampaign API key. Found in Settings → Developer → Key. Passed as Api-Token header on every request.

sections.title: Sync Engine
sections.content: The sync() method performs a full paginated sync of ActiveCampaign objects. For each object type (contacts, deals, campaigns), it fetches all pages using offset-based pagination. The total record count is read from the meta.total field in each API response. Each raw API record is passed through a normalizer that converts it into a ConnectorDocument with a stable source_id.

methods.description: Validates credentials by probing GET /api/3/users/me. Returns HEALTHY + CONNECTED on success, or OFFLINE + appropriate AuthStatus on failure. Sets http_client on success.

Pings GET /api/3/users/me. Returns HEALTHY (with user_name and user_email), DEGRADED (transient network error, circuit not open), or OFFLINE (auth failure or circuit open). Updates the circuit breaker state.

methods.signature: async def sync(full: bool = False, since: datetime | None = None, kb_id: str = '') -> SyncResult
methods.description: Paginated full sync of contacts, deals, and campaigns. Returns documents_found, documents_synced, documents_failed, and a SyncStatus (COMPLETED / PARTIAL / FAILED).

methods.signature: async def list_contacts(limit: int = 100, offset: int = 0) -> dict
methods.description: Returns a paginated page of ActiveCampaign contacts.
Returns a single ActiveCampaign contact by ID.

methods.signature: async def list_deals(limit: int = 100, offset: int = 0) -> dict
methods.description: Returns a paginated page of ActiveCampaign deals.
Returns a single ActiveCampaign deal by ID.

methods.signature: async def list_campaigns(limit: int = 100, offset: int = 0) -> dict
methods.description: Returns a paginated page of ActiveCampaign campaigns.

methods.signature: async def list_lists(limit: int = 100, offset: int = 0) -> dict
methods.description: Returns a paginated page of ActiveCampaign subscriber lists.
Closes the underlying httpx.AsyncClient and clears the http_client reference.

sections.title: Data Model
sections.description: Every ActiveCampaign object is normalized into a ConnectorDocument before being ingested into the Shielva knowledge base.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Human-readable title. Format varies by object type.

fields.name: content
fields.description: Newline-delimited key-value representation of the object's properties.

fields.name: source_url
fields.description: Empty string — AC does not have stable deep links.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Object-type-specific properties map. Always includes object_type and ac_id keys.

exceptions.name: ActiveCampaignError
exceptions.description: Base exception. Carries message, status_code, and code.

exceptions.name: ActiveCampaignAuthError
exceptions.description: Raised on 401 Unauthorized or 403 Forbidden. Not retried.

exceptions.name: ActiveCampaignRateLimitError
exceptions.description: Raised on 429 Too Many Requests. Carries retry_after (seconds from Retry-After header). Retried after the specified delay.

exceptions.name: ActiveCampaignNotFoundError
exceptions.description: Raised on 404 Not Found. Carries resource name and resource ID.

exceptions.name: ActiveCampaignNetworkError
exceptions.description: Raised on httpx.TimeoutException or httpx.NetworkError. Retried with exponential backoff.

exceptions.name: ActiveCampaignServerError
exceptions.description: Raised on 5xx responses from ActiveCampaign. Retried.

items.cause: API key is invalid, has been regenerated, or the account was deleted.
items.solution: Go to ActiveCampaign Settings → Developer, copy the current API key, and update the connector's api_key field.

items.cause: The account associated with the API key does not have permission to read the requested resource.
items.solution: Ensure the account is an admin or has the necessary role in ActiveCampaign.

items.cause: The api_url field contains a typo, includes /api/3/, or uses the wrong subdomain.
items.solution: Copy the exact URL from Settings → Developer. It should look like https://youraccountname.api-us1.com with no trailing path.
The connector retries automatically with exponential backoff honouring Retry-After. For very large accounts, schedule syncs during off-peak hours.

items.cause: Transient network errors have started tripping the circuit breaker (fewer than 5 failures).
items.solution: Resolve the underlying network issue and trigger a health check. The circuit breaker resets on a successful call.

items.cause: Authentication failure or circuit breaker has opened (5+ consecutive failures).
items.solution: Fix the root cause (auth or network), then trigger a health check to reset the circuit breaker.

items.cause: One or more records returned unexpected data that caused normalization to fail.
items.solution: Check the connector logs for which record IDs failed. Inspect those records in ActiveCampaign for corrupted or null property values.
